response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates 20+ years of strong software development experience and versatile technical skills, the resume reveals critical misalignments with this embedded security role. Most critically, the required native-level Japanese language proficiency is completely absent from the candidate's profile. Additionally, there is no evidence of security domain expertise (encryption, authentication systems, tamper detection), embedded firmware experience, or Linux kernel-level work—all core technical requirements. The candidate's career has focused on web, mobile, and high-level systems rather than embedded networking and security.
strengths: 20+ years of extensive software development experience across multiple platforms and languages
Strong emphasis on testing, TDD, and software craftsmanship with proven ability to build quality systems
Adaptability demonstrated through mastery of diverse tech stacks (D, TypeScript, Java, C++, etc.) and problem domains
concerns: No mention of native-level or any Japanese language proficiency, which is an explicit required qualification
Complete absence of security technologies expertise (encryption, authentication, MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, tamper detection)
No demonstrated experience with embedded firmware, Linux kernel work, or networking technologies (Layer 2/3, switches, SNMP, NETCONF)
